rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF LEVETY YURITSY CHAVEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The court-appointed attorney for the former minor has not petitioned for her fees and costs. 2) The guardian's current bond is insufficient and has not been increased. 3) The estate cash on hand is not fully insured. 4) The guardian requests fees for her rental property management services in the total amount of $10,507. 5) The guardian's attorneys request fees in the total amount of $15,240. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Monica Zimmerman For Terminating Sanctions And Other Sanctions, Or To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: (Part 1 of 2) This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Motion by Monica Zimmerman for Terminating Sanctions and Other Sanctions or to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories. The court GRANTS moving party's request for judicial notice. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) The court exercises it discretion in considering Rudolph Koppl's response to the motion in excess of 15 pages (see, CRC, rules 3.1113(g) and 3.1300). Moving party's request for an order imposing terminating sanctions by striking in its entirety Rudolph's Amended Petition to Interpret Trust, filed January 4, 2019 is DENIED. Moving party's request for an order imposing terminating sanctions by striking in its entirety the Response and Opposition by Beneficiary Rudolph Koppl to Petition to Determine Existence or Nonexistence of Power, Duty, or Right; to Determine to Whom Property Shall Pass or Be Delivered; and to Pass Upon the Acts of Frida Koppl, as Then Trustee, filed March 11, 2019 in DENIED. Moving party's request in the alternative for an order imposing issue sanctions by striking all thirteen affirmative defenses in the Response and Opposition is DENIED. Moving party's request in the alternative for an order imposing issue sanctions by striking all denials set forth in the Response and Opposition of material facts stated in Respondents' Petition to Determine Existence or Nonexistence of Power, Duty, or Right; to Determine to Whom Property Shall Pass or Be Delivered; and to Pass Upon the Acts of Frida Koppl, as Then Trustee, filed on November 27, 2018 is DENIED. Moving party's request in the alternative for an order imposing evidence sanctions by prohibiting Rudolph from introducing any evidence for the purpose of supporting any material allegation of his Amended Petition to Interpret Trust, filed January 4, 2019 is DENIED. Moving party's request "in the further alternative" for an order under Code of Civ. Proc. Section 2030.290 to compel Rudolph Koppl to provide further responses to Form Interrogatory no. 17.1 as to Requests for Admission nos. 31, 42, 43, and 46, contained in Rudolph Koppl's Further Response to Monica Zimmerman's First Set of Form Interrogatories, served on June 1, 2022, is DENIED. Moving party's request for monetary sanctions against Rudolph Koppl and his attorney Andrew Zimmerman under Code of Civ. Proc. Sections 2023.010 et seq and section 2030.300 is DENIED. The further responses to Form Interrogatory no. 17.1 as to Requests for Admission nos. 31, 42, 43, and 46, contained in Rudolph Koppl's Further Response to Monica Zimmerman's First Set of Form Interrogatories, served on June 1, 2022, complained of in this motion appear to be code-compliant. The motion's arguments otherwise are not persuasive. Rudolph Koppl's request for sanctions is DENIED. Rodolph Koppl is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. (Tentative ruling continues in Part 2 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Monica Zimmerman For Terminating And Other Sanctions, Or To Compel Further Responses To Specially Prepared Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: (Part 1 of 2) This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Motion by Monica Zimmerman for Terminating and Other Sanctions, or to Compel Further Responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories. The court GRANTS moving party's request for judicial notice. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) The court exercises it discretion in considering Rudolph Koppl's response to the motion in excess of 15 pages (see, CRC, rules 3.1113(g) and 3.1300). Moving party's request for an order imposing terminating sanctions by striking in its entirety Rudolph's Amended Petition to Interpret Trust, filed January 4, 2019 is DENIED. Moving party's request for an order imposing terminating sanctions by striking in its entirety the Response and Opposition by Beneficiary Rudolph Koppl to Petition to Determine Existence or Nonexistence of Power, Duty, or Right; to Determine to Whom Property Shall Pass or Be Delivered; and to Pass Upon the Acts of Frida Koppl, as Then Trustee, filed March 11, 2019 in DENIED. Moving party's request in the alternative for an order imposing issue sanctions by striking all thirteen affirmative defenses in the Response and Opposition is DENIED. Moving party's request in the alternative for an order imposing issue sanctions by striking all denials set forth in the Response and Opposition of material facts stated in Respondents' Petition to Determine Existence or Nonexistence of Power, Duty, or Right; to Determine to Whom Property Shall Pass or Be Delivered; and to Pass Upon the Acts of Frida Koppl, as Then Trustee, filed on November 27, 2018 is DENIED. Moving party's request in the alternative for an order imposing evidence sanctions by prohibiting Rudolph from introducing any evidence for the purpose of supporting any material allegation of his Amended Petition to Interpret Trust, filed January 4, 2019 is DENIED. Moving party's request "in the further alternative" for an order under Code of Civ. Proc. Section 2030.290 to compel Rudolph Koppl to provide further responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatory nos. 1, 3, 11, 12, 39, and 41, contained in Rudolph Koppl's Further Responses to Monica Zimmerman's First Set of Specially Prepared Interrogatories served on June 1, 2022, is DENIED. Moving party's request for monetary sanctions against Rudolph Koppl and his attorney Andrew Zimmerman under Code of Civ. Proc. Sections 2023.010 et seq and section 2030.300 is DENIED. The further responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatory nos. 1, 3, 11, 12, 39, and 41, contained in Rudolph Koppl's Further Responses to Monica Zimmerman's First Set of Specially Prepared Interrogatories served on June 1, 2022, complained of in this motion appear to be code-compliant. The motion's arguments otherwise are not persuasive. Rudolph Koppl's request for sanctions is DENIED. Rodolph Koppl is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. (Tentative ruling continues in Part 2 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAKO U ROVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine if the amended petition for Letters of Administration should be taken off calendar for failure to address issues after numerous continuances. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK.

rulings.Rulings: OFF CALENDAR. Petitioner dismissed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PRECIOCISIMA B PRIMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/13/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A petition may not be AMENDED by a supplement. [See CRC 7.3 for definitions and 7.53(a) re notice requirements.] Although notice was reportedly given of the "supplement" that amended the petition, no written consent by the other beneficiary was filed, affirming his consent to hold the real property in joint tenancy rather than as tenants-in-common. A revised proposed order has not been submitted, setting forth the specific assets to be distributed, as shown in the "supplement". A revised proposed order and a courtesy copy of the other beneficiary's written consent to taking title to the real property as a joint tenant must be received by the assigned examiner by 3/3/23. If the other beneficiary does not consent to taking the real property interest as a joint tenant, an amendment to the petition must be filed, requesting the real property be distributed to them as tenants-in-common. Notice of the amendment must be given after the amendment is filed, and a courtesy copy of the amendment and proof or service must be received by the examiner by 3/3/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT FISHER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT C. HAUNGS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. It appears, however, that the executor kept cash in a bank outside of the state of California. [Probate Code Section 9700.] NB: When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JESSE BYRD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Order Authorizing Particular Transaction
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/8/23. (No earlier availability.) Still no proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies and must be provided at least five (5) Court days before a hearing. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] The proposed order and a single-sided courtesy copy of the petition must be received by the assigned examiner by 2/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RONALD BALA ROMINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/23 for the following issues to be addressed by the petitioner: 1) Item 8 to be updated to include names of Decedent's parents and their dates of death. 2) Item 8 to be updated to include all predeceased sibling(s), along with date(s) of death, and their issue, and to confirm that there are no other siblings, alive or deceased. 3) Petitioner states in his 12/29 declaration that the objector and his siblings' relationship to Decedent has not been confirmed. Allegation needed as to whether confirmation has now been made, and if not, why it cannot be confirmed at this time. 4) Petitioner requests no bond be required as the other potential beneficiaries only have an interest in separate property; however, no authority (or calculation) has been provided to justify a complete waiver of bond or a reduction.
